title: Software and AI - Plotting vs. Pantsing
excerpt: An alt take on why some like or dislike AI
content_html:
18 July, 2026 - #ai

Table of Contents
    • Software "Plotting" and "Pantsing"
    • Delegation versus Assistance
    • Real Life Application
      • Working in Existing Code
      • Green Fields
    • Where to, from here?

I've been wondering why people seem to love AI for their work, while others hate it. My curiosity revolves around usage of AI in the work itself, setting aside social, economic and environmental concerns. The source of the divide might partly be how AI affects the way we build understanding of software and problem domains.

Fiction writing, like software, requires managing many details in a coherent way. There are two styles of fiction writing: plotters ("planners", "architects") who work through their ideas ahead of time, and pantsers ("gardeners") who write by the "seat of their pants." Authors use both of these at different times but might favor one or the other. To avoid the misinterpretation that people only fit into one category or another, I'll refer to the behavior of these approaches as "plotting" and "pantsing."

Plotting in fiction writing is worldbuilding, writing character guides, and outlining the major story beats and arc before starting to write. Pantsing would be taking a few interesting characters and a setting, and just starting to write, figuring where the story ends up as you go.

These two approaches seem to mirror some software practices and approaches. My goal isn't to promote or denigrate either style, but rather to use this as a framework to think about software engineering and how it impacts AI usage.

Software "Plotting" and "Pantsing"

"Plotting" would be thinking through and developing a solution before any code is written. "Pantsing" would be treating the code as a main artifact and using that to think through the solution.

It's not perfect, but plotting emphasizes a top-down analytical form of construction, whereas pantsing focuses on creating from bottom-up synthesis.

The difference is how code is treated and where understanding lives. In plotting, code is an expression of the design, whereas in pantsing, it is the design.

For some people like myself, writing the code helps us build our understanding of the problem. Telling someone to have an AI write all the code isn't "taking away our work," it's taking away our understanding. When people talk about AI stealing the joy of their craft, this might be why, which would explain some of the divide in AI adoption.

Delegation versus Assistance

This seems to explain why AI usage differs in ease and usefulness. AI usage terminology seems quite blurry, so I'll define what I mean while using the umbrella term "AI" for these systems. The distinction doesn't split neatly between "agents" and inline editing or chat systems.

For plotting versus pantsing, the divide would be the autonomy and granularity with which AI updates artifacts. "Delegates" would be an AI which is given a goal and allowed to autonomously perform edits on the work products. "Assistants" would encompass the rest of work in which the human maintains control.

This would suggest that delegates support plotting, while assistants support pantsing. A delegate goes from high level specifications directly to a shipped product. An assistant collaborates with the human, such as for autocomplete, research, or code review. It's not important whether these happen with agents or a chat interface, but how their end products get used.

With definitions set up, how does this apply?

Real Life Application

Initially I thought plotting and pantsing might appeal to specific domains, but it seems to align with different types of work and levels of knowledge of the system.

Working in Existing Code

Many software developers work on projects that they themselves didn't write. Few programmers write main() and developers tenure on projects tends to be very short. It's also not uncommon to find a team with no original product authors left, but the business case for the software didn't leave. In this environment, the only way to learn how the software works might be to read the code and tests. This sounds straightforward, but mature systems surviving evolutionary development often have vestigial constraints and requirements. When dealing with older code, you need to learn the current system state. This gets worse when dealing with millions of lines of code and with intercommunicating and disparate systems, such as microservices created by different teams.

I've been a fresh face on existing codebases many times. There are often portions where human memory or departures result in unmaintained code whose design decisions or desired architecture are lost to time. That weird acronym that makes no sense? Yeah, its meaning is lost to time. For extremely large programs (>1 million lines of code), it's impossible to maintain a full understanding of the whole system. If you take a software engineer and parachute them into this system, they're starting from zero and running mostly in pantsing mode. The system is a pile of unknowns.

  • What's the architecture?
  • How is CI/CD set up?
  • What checks help ensure the project runs correctly?
  • Who depends on this code and how will I know if I broke something?

Once you've developed an understanding of the systems on which you're assigned, you can plot changes usually in a straightforward way. Some systems have strong conventions, which helps enforce standard data flows and workflows. However, this isn't true for bespoke software, or in-house expertise, where you need to come up to speed with the vernacular. Coming up to speed often starts with little changes to get familiar with the workflows. Then you expand what you do as your understanding of the workflows expands. Knowledge gets retained and spread through the team as you work on different things.

In existing systems, constraints often necessitate pantsing until you're up to speed. New contributors also discover additional constraints or help sharpen the product by codifying unclear design elements they run across. When AI delegates get used for work, little or no shared understanding of the system gets built.

When I hear "AI isn't improving efficiency for developers," this is where my mind goes. If you don't know the current state of the program, or the current problem itself, it's difficult to delegate work. Using "delegate" CLI-based AI to go from spec to output doesn't help with the underlying problem. When you're lost, the bottleneck to delegation is understanding. You can go into planning mode and then iterate a bunch trying to get an understanding of the systems you touch, but this only informs you on the interface boundary between new changes and existing code.

In existing code, an "assistant" AI ensures a PR follows code conventions. Autocomplete would recommend using the appropriate internal types and vernacular. An assistant can also help find program areas to look at to get started on a problem, but this augments understanding and doesn't replace it.

Green Fields

Developing a greenfield project provides a completely different experience. Stability falls behind features as the product need might not be complete yet. Codifying understanding of the problem proves less important than demonstrating value.

A limited runway necessitates speed, which means a subdivision of work. Most problems aren't solved yet and there are similarly fewer internal constraints needing to be maintained. Entire systems might be tossed out if they prove unusable or the product pivots. Code is an expendable asset in green fields because it provides capability. Code is a liability in brown fields because it constrains.

Greenfield projects slowly convert into brownfield ones as they age and solidify. Knowledge slowly converts from being only mental through an imperfect filter into code. Some things cannot be expressed directly though, which leads to some loss.

An AI delegate writing greenfield code is less risky since there are fewer possible conflicts. There's less code written by others and the authors know why things are being done and the history of the project. You skip the pantsing to learn the product and can jump straight into plotting with little or no code in your way.

Problems require some sort of expression in code to run on a computer, and an infinite number of programs can produce the same output. There's a wild difference between knowing how to solve a problem and expressing it in a way which is fast or prevents correctness mistakes. When dealing with an unfamiliar problem, some people, like myself, iterate on various ways of expressing solutions to that problem. Some of these can be clever and useful, like the ideas described in Parse, don't validate. This is pantsing of a different sort, of using code to learn about the problem. It's a sort of play, a source of joy in programming and how we gain experience. When I hear people describe AI stealing the joy of programming, this removal of "play to learn" is what it means to me. The process produces not just a product, but also knowledge.

Using an AI to generate a solution removes part of the rationale for this type of work. Inline code generation and research lets you move faster though by reducing obstacles. An assistant for code review though provides backup and additional insights.

Where to, from here?

The knowledge of the system exists somewhere, whether in someone's head, or in a spec or in the code. Plotting maximizes the push of existing mental models into the world. Pantsing extracts mental models from the world and builds experience.

The critical issue is how AI usage affects the way software engineers think. These styles work together at various times for various reasons. A delegate AI converts existing understanding into code. Assistant AIs help convert documentation and code into understanding.

Using the wrong AI style can actively detract from the work being done. The important element seems to be using the right form of AI to support the type of development you're doing right now. Forcing a certain style of AI feels awkward, especially if it comes from someone above you.

Are you plotting or pantsing right now, and how does AI support that?

This sounds straightforward, but mature systems surviving evolutionary development often have vestigial constraints and requirements. When dealing with older code, you need to learn the current system state. This gets worse when dealing with millions of lines of code and with intercommunicating and disparate systems, such as microservices created by different teams.

sentences.en: Many software developers work on projects that they themselves didn't write.
sentences.zh: 许多软件开发人员从事的项目并不是他们自己编写的。

sentences.en: Few programmers write main() and developers tenure on projects tends to be very short.
sentences.zh: 很少有程序员编写 main() 并且开发人员在项目上的任期往往很短。

sentences.en: It's also not uncommon to find a team with no original product authors left, but the business case for the software didn't leave.
sentences.zh: 团队中没有原始产品作者的情况也很常见，但软件的业务案例却没有离开。

sentences.en: In this environment, the only way to learn how the software works might be to read the code and tests.
sentences.zh: 在这种环境中，了解软件如何工作的唯一方法可能是阅读代码和测试。

sentences.en: This sounds straightforward, but mature systems surviving evolutionary development often have vestigial constraints and requirements.
sentences.zh: 这听起来很简单，但在进化发展中幸存下来的成熟系统通常具有残留的约束和要求。

sentences.en: When dealing with older code, you need to learn the current system state.
sentences.zh: 在处理较旧的代码时，您需要了解当前的系统状态。

sentences.en: This gets worse when dealing with millions of lines of code and with intercommunicating and disparate systems, such as microservices created by different teams.
sentences.zh: 当处理数百万行代码以及相互通信和不同的系统（例如不同团队创建的微服务）时，情况会变得更糟。

blocks.html_en: I've been a fresh face on existing codebases many times. There are often portions where human memory or departures result in unmaintained code whose design decisions or desired architecture are lost to time. That weird acronym that makes no sense? Yeah, its meaning is lost to time. For extremely large programs (>1 million lines of code), it's impossible to maintain a full understanding of the whole system. If you take a software engineer and parachute them into this system, they're starting from zero and running mostly in pantsing mode. The system is a pile of unknowns.

sentences.en: I've been a fresh face on existing codebases many times.
sentences.zh: 我多次对现有代码库感到陌生。

sentences.en: There are often portions where human memory or departures result in unmaintained code whose design decisions or desired architecture are lost to time.
sentences.zh: 通常，人类记忆或偏离的某些部分会导致代码无法维护，而这些代码的设计决策或所需的架构会随着时间的推移而丢失。

sentences.en: That weird acronym that makes no sense?
sentences.zh: 那个毫无意义的奇怪缩写？

sentences.en: Yeah, its meaning is lost to time.
sentences.zh: 是的，它的意义已经随着时间而消失了。

sentences.en: For extremely large programs (>1 million lines of code), it's impossible to maintain a full understanding of the whole system.
sentences.zh: 对于非常大的程序（>100万行代码），不可能保持对整个系统的完全理解。

sentences.en: If you take a software engineer and parachute them into this system, they're starting from zero and running mostly in pantsing mode.
sentences.zh: 如果你将一名软件工程师降落到这个系统中，他们将从零开始，并且主要以喘气的模式运行。

sentences.en: The system is a pile of unknowns.
sentences.zh: 系统是一堆未知数。

blocks.html_en: • What's the architecture?

sentences.en: What's the architecture?
sentences.zh: 架构是什么？

blocks.html_en: • How is CI/CD set up?

sentences.en: How is CI/CD set up?
sentences.zh: CI/CD 是如何设置的？

blocks.html_en: • What checks help ensure the project runs correctly?

sentences.en: What checks help ensure the project runs correctly?
sentences.zh: 哪些检查有助于确保项目正确运行？

blocks.html_en: • Who depends on this code and how will I know if I broke something?

sentences.en: Who depends on this code and how will I know if I broke something?
sentences.zh: 谁依赖此代码？我如何知道我是否破坏了某些内容？

blocks.html_en: Once you've developed an understanding of the systems on which you're assigned, you can plot changes usually in a straightforward way. Some systems have strong conventions, which helps enforce standard data flows and workflows. However, this isn't true for bespoke software, or in-house expertise, where you need to come up to speed with the vernacular. Coming up to speed often starts with little changes to get familiar with the workflows. Then you expand what you do as your understanding of the workflows expands. Knowledge gets retained and spread through the team as you work on different things.

sentences.en: Once you've developed an understanding of the systems on which you're assigned, you can plot changes usually in a straightforward way.
sentences.zh: 一旦您了解了分配给您的系统，您通常可以以简单的方式绘制变化图。

sentences.en: Some systems have strong conventions, which helps enforce standard data flows and workflows.
sentences.zh: 有些系统具有强大的约定，这有助于强制执行标准的数据流和工作流程。

sentences.en: However, this isn't true for bespoke software, or in-house expertise, where you need to come up to speed with the vernacular.
sentences.zh: 然而，对于定制软件或内部专业知识来说，情况并非如此，您需要跟上语言的速度。

sentences.en: Coming up to speed often starts with little changes to get familiar with the workflows.
sentences.zh: 加快速度通常是从一些小的改变开始，以熟悉工作流程。

sentences.en: Then you expand what you do as your understanding of the workflows expands.
sentences.zh: 然后，随着您对工作流程的理解的加深，您可以扩展您所做的事情。

sentences.en: Knowledge gets retained and spread through the team as you work on different things.
sentences.zh: 当您从事不同的事情时，知识会在团队中保留和传播。

blocks.html_en: In existing systems, constraints often necessitate pantsing until you're up to speed. New contributors also discover additional constraints or help sharpen the product by codifying unclear design elements they run across. When AI delegates get used for work, little or no shared understanding of the system gets built.

sentences.en: In existing systems, constraints often necessitate pantsing until you're up to speed.
sentences.zh: 在现有的系统中，约束常常需要你在跟上速度之前必须喘口气。

sentences.en: New contributors also discover additional constraints or help sharpen the product by codifying unclear design elements they run across.
sentences.zh: 新的贡献者还发现了额外的限制，或者通过编写他们遇到的不明确的设计元素来帮助改进产品。

sentences.en: When AI delegates get used for work, little or no shared understanding of the system gets built.
sentences.zh: 当人工智能代表习惯于工作时，很少或根本没有建立对系统的共同理解。

blocks.html_en: When I hear "AI isn't improving efficiency for developers," this is where my mind goes. If you don't know the current state of the program, or the current problem itself, it's difficult to delegate work. Using "delegate" CLI-based AI to go from spec to output doesn't help with the underlying problem. When you're lost, the bottleneck to delegation is understanding. You can go into planning mode and then iterate a bunch trying to get an understanding of the systems you touch, but this only informs you on the interface boundary between new changes and existing code.

sentences.en: When I hear "AI isn't improving efficiency for developers," this is where my mind goes.
sentences.zh: 当我听到“人工智能并没有提高开发人员的效率”时，我的脑海里浮现出这样的想法。

sentences.en: If you don't know the current state of the program, or the current problem itself, it's difficult to delegate work.
sentences.zh: 如果您不知道程序的当前状态或当前问题本身，则很难委派工作。

sentences.en: Using "delegate" CLI-based AI to go from spec to output doesn't help with the underlying problem.
sentences.zh: 使用“委托”基于 CLI 的 AI 从规范到输出无助于解决根本问题。

sentences.en: When you're lost, the bottleneck to delegation is understanding.
sentences.zh: 当你迷失时，授权的瓶颈就是理解。

sentences.en: You can go into planning mode and then iterate a bunch trying to get an understanding of the systems you touch, but this only informs you on the interface boundary between new changes and existing code.
sentences.zh: 您可以进入计划模式，然后迭代一系列尝试来了解您接触的系统，但这只能告诉您新更改和现有代码之间的接口边界。

blocks.html_en: In existing code, an "assistant" AI ensures a PR follows code conventions. Autocomplete would recommend using the appropriate internal types and vernacular. An assistant can also help find program areas to look at to get started on a problem, but this augments understanding and doesn't replace it.

sentences.en: In existing code, an "assistant" AI ensures a PR follows code conventions.
sentences.zh: 在现有代码中，“助理”人工智能可确保 PR 遵循代码约定。

sentences.en: Autocomplete would recommend using the appropriate internal types and vernacular.
sentences.zh: 自动完成功能会建议使用适当的内部类型和白话。

sentences.en: An assistant can also help find program areas to look at to get started on a problem, but this augments understanding and doesn't replace it.
sentences.zh: 助理还可以帮助找到要查看的程序领域以开始解决问题，但这会增强理解，但不会取代它。

blocks.html_en: Green Fields

sentences.en: Green Fields
sentences.zh: 绿色田野

blocks.html_en: Developing a greenfield project provides a completely different experience. Stability falls behind features as the product need might not be complete yet. Codifying understanding of the problem proves less important than demonstrating value.

sentences.en: Developing a greenfield project provides a completely different experience.
sentences.zh: 开发绿地项目提供了完全不同的体验。

sentences.en: Stability falls behind features as the product need might not be complete yet.
sentences.zh: 由于产品需求可能尚未完成，稳定性落后于功能。

sentences.en: Codifying understanding of the problem proves less important than demonstrating value.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，对问题的理解进行编码并不比展示价值更重要。

blocks.html_en: A limited runway necessitates speed, which means a subdivision of work. Most problems aren't solved yet and there are similarly fewer internal constraints needing to be maintained. Entire systems might be tossed out if they prove unusable or the product pivots. Code is an expendable asset in green fields because it provides capability. Code is a liability in brown fields because it constrains.

sentences.en: A limited runway necessitates speed, which means a subdivision of work.
sentences.zh: 有限的跑道需要速度，这意味着工作的细分。

sentences.en: Most problems aren't solved yet and there are similarly fewer internal constraints needing to be maintained.
sentences.zh: 大多数问题尚未解决，同样需要维护的内部约束也较少。

sentences.en: Entire systems might be tossed out if they prove unusable or the product pivots.
sentences.zh: 如果整个系统被证明无法使用或产品发生变化，则可能会被抛弃。

sentences.en: Code is an expendable asset in green fields because it provides capability.
sentences.zh: 代码是绿色领域的消耗性资产，因为它提供了功能。

sentences.en: Code is a liability in brown fields because it constrains.
sentences.zh: 代码在棕色领域是一种负担，因为它具有约束力。

blocks.html_en: Greenfield projects slowly convert into brownfield ones as they age and solidify. Knowledge slowly converts from being only mental through an imperfect filter into code. Some things cannot be expressed directly though, which leads to some loss.

sentences.en: Greenfield projects slowly convert into brownfield ones as they age and solidify.
sentences.zh: 随着老化和固化，绿地项目慢慢转变为棕地项目。

sentences.en: Knowledge slowly converts from being only mental through an imperfect filter into code.
sentences.zh: 知识通过不完美的过滤器慢慢地从精神上转化为代码。

sentences.en: Some things cannot be expressed directly though, which leads to some loss.
sentences.zh: 有些事情虽然不能直接表达，但会造成一些损失。

blocks.html_en: An AI delegate writing greenfield code is less risky since there are fewer possible conflicts. There's less code written by others and the authors know why things are being done and the history of the project. You skip the pantsing to learn the product and can jump straight into plotting with little or no code in your way.

sentences.en: An AI delegate writing greenfield code is less risky since there are fewer possible conflicts.
sentences.zh: 人工智能代表编写新代码的风险较小，因为可能的冲突较少。

sentences.en: There's less code written by others and the authors know why things are being done and the history of the project.
sentences.zh: 其他人编写的代码较少，而且作者知道为什么要做这些事情以及项目的历史。

sentences.en: You skip the pantsing to learn the product and can jump straight into plotting with little or no code in your way.
sentences.zh: 您可以跳过学习该产品的过程，而可以直接跳入绘图，而无需编写任何代码。

blocks.html_en: Problems require some sort of expression in code to run on a computer, and an infinite number of programs can produce the same output. There's a wild difference between knowing how to solve a problem and expressing it in a way which is fast or prevents correctness mistakes. When dealing with an unfamiliar problem, some people, like myself, iterate on various ways of expressing solutions to that problem. Some of these can be clever and useful, like the ideas described in Parse, don't validate. This is pantsing of a different sort, of using code to learn about the problem. It's a sort of play, a source of joy in programming and how we gain experience. When I hear people describe AI stealing the joy of programming, this removal of "play to learn" is what it means to me. The process produces not just a product, but also knowledge.

sentences.en: Problems require some sort of expression in code to run on a computer, and an infinite number of programs can produce the same output.
sentences.zh: 问题需要某种代码表达才能在计算机上运行，​​并且无数的程序可以产生相同的输出。

sentences.en: There's a wild difference between knowing how to solve a problem and expressing it in a way which is fast or prevents correctness mistakes.
sentences.zh: 知道如何解决问题与以快速或防止正确性错误的方式表达问题之间存在巨大差异。

sentences.en: When dealing with an unfamiliar problem, some people, like myself, iterate on various ways of expressing solutions to that problem.
sentences.zh: 在处理一个不熟悉的问题时，有些人，比如我自己，会反复使用各种方式来表达该问题的解决方案。

sentences.en: Some of these can be clever and useful, like the ideas described in Parse, don't validate.
sentences.zh: 其中一些可能很聪明且有用，例如 Parse 中描述的想法，但无需验证。

sentences.en: This is pantsing of a different sort, of using code to learn about the problem.
sentences.zh: 这是另一种渴望，即使用代码来了解问题。

sentences.en: It's a sort of play, a source of joy in programming and how we gain experience.
sentences.zh: 这是一种游戏，是编程乐趣的源泉，也是我们获得经验的方式。

sentences.en: When I hear people describe AI stealing the joy of programming, this removal of "play to learn" is what it means to me.
sentences.zh: 当我听到人们描述人工智能窃取了编程的乐趣时，这种“边玩边学”的消除对我来说意味着什么。

sentences.en: The process produces not just a product, but also knowledge.
sentences.zh: 这个过程不仅产生产品，而且产生知识。

blocks.html_en: Using an AI to generate a solution removes part of the rationale for this type of work. Inline code generation and research lets you move faster though by reducing obstacles. An assistant for code review though provides backup and additional insights.

sentences.en: Using an AI to generate a solution removes part of the rationale for this type of work.
sentences.zh: 使用人工智能生成解决方案消除了此类工作的部分原理。

sentences.en: Inline code generation and research lets you move faster though by reducing obstacles.
sentences.zh: 内联代码生成和研究可以通过减少障碍来加快您的步伐。

sentences.en: An assistant for code review though provides backup and additional insights.
sentences.zh: 代码审查助手提供了备份和额外的见解。

blocks.html_en: Where to, from here?

sentences.en: Where to, from here?
sentences.zh: 从这里去哪里？

blocks.html_en: The knowledge of the system exists somewhere, whether in someone's head, or in a spec or in the code. Plotting maximizes the push of existing mental models into the world. Pantsing extracts mental models from the world and builds experience.

sentences.en: The knowledge of the system exists somewhere, whether in someone's head, or in a spec or in the code.
sentences.zh: 系统的知识存在于某个地方，无论是在某人的头脑中，还是在规范或代码中。

sentences.en: Plotting maximizes the push of existing mental models into the world.
sentences.zh: 绘图可以最大限度地将现有的心理模型推向现实世界。

sentences.en: Pantsing extracts mental models from the world and builds experience.
sentences.zh: Pantsing 从世界中提取心理模型并积累经验。

blocks.html_en: The critical issue is how AI usage affects the way software engineers think. These styles work together at various times for various reasons. A delegate AI converts existing understanding into code. Assistant AIs help convert documentation and code into understanding.

sentences.en: The critical issue is how AI usage affects the way software engineers think.
sentences.zh: 关键问题是人工智能的使用如何影响软件工程师的思维方式。

sentences.en: These styles work together at various times for various reasons.
sentences.zh: 由于各种原因，这些风格在不同的时间一起发挥作用。

sentences.en: A delegate AI converts existing understanding into code.
sentences.zh: 人工智能代表将现有的理解转化为代码。

sentences.en: Assistant AIs help convert documentation and code into understanding.
sentences.zh: 人工智能助理帮助将文档和代码转化为理解。

blocks.html_en: Using the wrong AI style can actively detract from the work being done. The important element seems to be using the right form of AI to support the type of development you're doing right now. Forcing a certain style of AI feels awkward, especially if it comes from someone above you.

sentences.en: Using the wrong AI style can actively detract from the work being done.
sentences.zh: 使用错误的人工智能风格可能会严重影响正在完成的工作。

sentences.en: The important element seems to be using the right form of AI to support the type of development you're doing right now.
sentences.zh: 重要的因素似乎是使用正确的人工智能形式来支持您现在正在进行的开发类型。

sentences.en: Forcing a certain style of AI feels awkward, especially if it comes from someone above you.
sentences.zh: 强制采用某种人工智能风格会让人感觉很尴尬，尤其是当它来自于你之上的人时。

blocks.html_en: Are you plotting or pantsing right now, and how does AI support that?

sentences.en: Are you plotting or pantsing right now, and how does AI support that?
sentences.zh: 你现在是在密谋还是在焦急，人工智能如何支持这一点？

content_html_zh:
2026 年 7 月 18 日 - #ai

Table of Contents
    • 软件“绘图”和“裤子”
    • 授权与协助
    • 现实生活中的应用
      • 使用现有代码
      • 绿色田野
    • 从这里去哪里？

我一直想知道为什么人们似乎喜欢人工智能的工作，而其他人则讨厌它。 我的好奇心围绕着人工智能在工作本身的使用，抛开社会、经济和环境问题。 造成这种分歧的部分原因可能是人工智能如何影响我们理解软件和问题领域的方式。

小说写作就像软件一样，需要以连贯的方式管理许多细节。 小说写作有两种风格：策划者（“策划者”、“建筑师”）提前构思自己的想法，以及裤子制作者（“园丁”）凭“感觉”写作。 作者在不同时间使用这两种方法，但可能偏爱其中一种。 为了避免人们只适合某一类别的误解，我将这些方法的行为称为“阴谋”和“裤子”。

小说写作中的情节是在开始写作之前构建世界、编写人物指南并概述主要故事情节和弧线。 Pantsing 会选择一些有趣的角色和背景，然后开始写作，边走边猜想故事的结局。

这两种方法似乎反映了一些软件实践和方法。 我的目标不是推广或诋毁这两种风格，而是将其用作思考软件工程及其如何影响人工智能使用的框架。

软件“绘图”和“裤子”

“绘图”是在编写任何代码之前思考并开发解决方案。 “Pantsing”会将代码视为主要工件，并使用它来思考解决方案。

它并不完美，但绘图强调自上而下的分析结构形式，而裤子则侧重于自下而上的综合创造。

区别在于如何处理代码以及理解的所在。 在绘图中，代码是设计的表达，而在裤子中，代码就是设计。

对于像我这样的人来说，编写代码可以帮助我们建立对问题的理解。 告诉某人让人工智能编写所有代码并不是“夺走我们的工作”，而是夺走了我们的理解。 当人们谈论人工智能窃取了他们的工艺乐趣时，这可能就是原因，这可以解释人工智能采用方面的一些分歧。

授权与协助

这似乎解释了为什么人工智能的使用在易用性和实用性方面存在差异。 人工智能使用术语似乎相当模糊，因此我将在为这些系统使用总括术语“人工智能”时定义我的意思。 “代理”和内联编辑或聊天系统之间的区别并没有明确划分。

对于绘图与裤子来说，区别在于人工智能更新工件的自主性和粒度。 “代表”将是一个人工智能，它被赋予一个目标并允许自主地对工作产品进行编辑。 “助手”将涵盖人类保持控制的其余工作。

这表明代表们支持密谋，而助理则支持胡作非为。 代表从高级规范直接到已发货的产品。 助理与人类协作，例如自动完成、研究或代码审查。 这些是否发生在代理或聊天界面上并不重要，重要的是他们的最终产品如何使用。

定义完成后，它如何应用？

现实生活中的应用

最初，我认为绘图和裤子可能会吸引特定领域，但它似乎与不同类型的工作和系统知识水平保持一致。

使用现有代码

许多软件开发人员从事的项目并不是他们自己编写的。 很少有程序员编写 main() 并且开发人员在项目上的任期往往很短。 团队中没有原始产品作者的情况也很常见，但软件的业务案例却没有离开。 在这种环境中，了解软件如何工作的唯一方法可能是阅读代码和测试。 这听起来很简单，但在进化发展中幸存下来的成熟系统通常具有残留的约束和要求。 在处理较旧的代码时，您需要了解当前的系统状态。 当处理数百万行代码以及相互通信和不同的系统（例如不同团队创建的微服务）时，情况会变得更糟。

我多次对现有代码库感到陌生。 通常，人类记忆或偏离的某些部分会导致代码无法维护，而这些代码的设计决策或所需的架构会随着时间的推移而丢失。 那个毫无意义的奇怪缩写？ 是的，它的意义已经随着时间而消失了。 对于非常大的程序（>100万行代码），不可能保持对整个系统的完全理解。 如果你将一名软件工程师降落到这个系统中，他们将从零开始，并且主要以喘气的模式运行。 系统是一堆未知数。

  • 架构是什么？
  • CI/CD 是如何设置的？
  • 哪些检查有助于确保项目正确运行？
  • 谁依赖此代码？我如何知道我是否破坏了某些内容？

一旦您了解了分配给您的系统，您通常可以以简单的方式绘制变化图。 有些系统具有强大的约定，这有助于强制执行标准的数据流和工作流程。 然而，对于定制软件或内部专业知识来说，情况并非如此，您需要跟上语言的速度。 加快速度通常是从一些小的改变开始，以熟悉工作流程。 然后，随着您对工作流程的理解的加深，您可以扩展您所做的事情。 当您从事不同的事情时，知识会在团队中保留和传播。

在现有的系统中，约束常常需要你在跟上速度之前必须喘口气。 新的贡献者还发现了额外的限制，或者通过编写他们遇到的不明确的设计元素来帮助改进产品。 当人工智能代表习惯于工作时，很少或根本没有建立对系统的共同理解。

当我听到“人工智能并没有提高开发人员的效率”时，我的脑海里浮现出这样的想法。 如果您不知道程序的当前状态或当前问题本身，则很难委派工作。 使用“委托”基于 CLI 的 AI 从规范到输出无助于解决根本问题。 当你迷失时，授权的瓶颈就是理解。 您可以进入计划模式，然后迭代一系列尝试来了解您接触的系统，但这只能告诉您新更改和现有代码之间的接口边界。

在现有代码中，“助理”人工智能可确保 PR 遵循代码约定。 自动完成功能会建议使用适当的内部类型和白话。 助理还可以帮助找到要查看的程序领域以开始解决问题，但这会增强理解，但不会取代它。

绿色田野

开发绿地项目提供了完全不同的体验。 由于产品需求可能尚未完成，稳定性落后于功能。 事实证明，对问题的理解进行编码并不比展示价值更重要。

有限的跑道需要速度，这意味着工作的细分。 大多数问题尚未解决，同样需要维护的内部约束也较少。 如果整个系统被证明无法使用或产品发生变化，则可能会被抛弃。 代码是绿色领域的消耗性资产，因为它提供了功能。 代码在棕色领域是一种负担，因为它具有约束力。

随着老化和固化，绿地项目慢慢转变为棕地项目。 知识通过不完美的过滤器慢慢地从精神上转化为代码。 有些事情虽然不能直接表达，但会造成一些损失。

人工智能代表编写新代码的风险较小，因为可能的冲突较少。 其他人编写的代码较少，而且作者知道为什么要做这些事情以及项目的历史。 您可以跳过学习该产品的过程，而可以直接跳入绘图，而无需编写任何代码。

问题需要某种代码表达才能在计算机上运行，​​并且无数的程序可以产生相同的输出。 知道如何解决问题与以快速或防止正确性错误的方式表达问题之间存在巨大差异。 在处理一个不熟悉的问题时，有些人，比如我自己，会反复使用各种方式来表达该问题的解决方案。 其中一些可能很聪明且有用，例如 Parse 中描述的想法，但无需验证。 这是另一种渴望，即使用代码来了解问题。 这是一种游戏，是编程乐趣的源泉，也是我们获得经验的方式。 当我听到人们描述人工智能窃取了编程的乐趣时，这种“边玩边学”的消除对我来说意味着什么。 这个过程不仅产生产品，而且产生知识。

使用人工智能生成解决方案消除了此类工作的部分原理。 内联代码生成和研究可以通过减少障碍来加快您的步伐。 代码审查助手提供了备份和额外的见解。

从这里去哪里？

系统的知识存在于某个地方，无论是在某人的头脑中，还是在规范或代码中。 绘图可以最大限度地将现有的心理模型推向现实世界。 Pantsing 从世界中提取心理模型并积累经验。

关键问题是人工智能的使用如何影响软件工程师的思维方式。 由于各种原因，这些风格在不同的时间一起发挥作用。 人工智能代表将现有的理解转化为代码。 人工智能助理帮助将文档和代码转化为理解。

使用错误的人工智能风格可能会严重影响正在完成的工作。 重要的因素似乎是使用正确的人工智能形式来支持您现在正在进行的开发类型。 强制采用某种人工智能风格会让人感觉很尴尬，尤其是当它来自于你之上的人时。

你现在是在密谋还是在焦急，人工智能如何支持这一点？
